ideas.title: AI can read your money — but do not fire your accountant
tags: Claude just killed the Finance Industry
ideas.about: Use the Claude finance-agent wave to teach business owners how AI can help them prepare better questions before talking to their bookkeeper, CPA, or financial advisor. The angle is safe, useful, and trust-building: AI is not replacing the expert; it is helping you show up smarter.
ideas.evidence: nocode.joshua's May 16 finance-agent post is the strongest current competitor signal in the scrape: **512,000 views / 24,500 saves / 4.78% save**. His on-screen title was `Claude just killed the Finance Industry`; spoken hook: “Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance AI agents...” That proves demand, but Braydon should take the more credible mentor angle.
ideas.spokenHook: “AI should not replace your accountant. But it can make your next accountant meeting ten times better.”
ideas.why: It wave-rides the biggest current competitor post while differentiating Braydon from the fear-based “killed finance” frame. Frank understands money, accountants, and better questions immediately. This also fits Braydon's positioning: AI empowers operators and experts instead of replacing judgment.

ideas.title: Claude plus Canva is becoming a design department for one-person businesses
ideas.about: Turn the Claude + Canva connector into a practical local-business demo: flyers, event graphics, social posts, carousel drafts, and brand-consistent assets created from one prompt. Do not frame it as “make pretty graphics.” Frame it as “your tiny business can finally move like it has a design department.”
ideas.evidence: Sabrina has a fresh May 17 Claude + Canva cluster: **41.9K views / 2,934 saves / 7.00% save** with on-screen title `Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯`, and **24.9K / 2,006 saves / 8.06% save** with `Claude + Canva is a literal cheatcode 😭😭😭`. Braydon's own Canva post from 10:12 AM has the right raw material: `Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯`, 640 views, 23 saves, 3.59% save while still early.
ideas.spokenHook: “If you make flyers, carousels, menus, or event graphics, Claude just became way more useful.”
ideas.why: This stacks two signals: Sabrina's fresh competitor wave and Braydon's own proven visual-output lane. It should be shot as a visible before/after demo, not a connector announcement. Show the local-business asset appearing on screen.

ideas.title: The one context file that makes Claude stop giving generic answers
tags: This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately
ideas.about: Reframe the underperforming “Claude memory/context” topic around a concrete business asset: one simple context file that tells Claude who you are, who your customer is, what you sell, your tone, your offers, and what good output looks like.
ideas.evidence: Taki's May 11 context-file post is still a strong recent signal at **18.1K views / 775 saves / 4.28% save**, on-screen title `This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately`. Braydon's own Claude memory post underperformed at **2,252 / 1.20% save**, which means the topic is useful but the first version likely did not make the payoff tangible enough.
ideas.spokenHook: “If Claude keeps giving you generic answers, build this one file before you write another prompt.”
ideas.why: This is a V2/reframe, not a lazy carry-over. The new evidence is that Taki's clearer “one file” framing is outperforming Braydon's broader memory framing. Make it a simple asset viewers can copy, not a conceptual explanation of context.

ideas.title: Claude can make the thing, but your taste still matters
ideas.about: Use Braydon's winning image/video posts to teach the expert-in-the-loop principle in plain English. The point is not that AI makes creative judgment irrelevant. The point is that AI gives operators more drafts, more options, and faster iteration — but the human still has to choose what is good.
ideas.evidence: Braydon's two strongest current posts are both creative-output demos: **167.7K / 5.03% save** on Claude image assets and **156.4K / 5.71% save** on cartoon videos. Rachel Woods has the operator-language pattern: “You don't need a human in the loop. You need an expert in the loop.” Her current post is early and small, so use it as language inspiration, not primary proof.
ideas.spokenHook: “Claude can make the asset now. But it still needs someone with taste to know which version is actually good.”
ideas.why: This gives Braydon a booker-friendly thought leadership clip while still tying back to his biggest performing lane. It is not as reach-proven as the finance or Canva ideas, but it deepens the brand and explains why business owners still matter.

ideas.title: Codex mobile is not about coding on your phone
ideas.about: If Braydon wants to revisit Codex mobile, change the frame completely. The value is not “code on a tiny screen.” The value is “send work forward while you are away from your desk.”
ideas.evidence: Braydon's first Codex mobile post is weak at **665 views / 4 saves / 0.60% save**, so this is not a top-priority reach play. But Marcinteodoru has a May 17 Codex-mobile-adjacent hook inside a beginner Claude post, and Braydon's earlier `Claude Code + Codex` post reached **31.3K**. The topic can work only if the use case is operator-clear.
ideas.onScreenTitle: `Codex can work while you are gone`
ideas.spokenHook: “The point of Codex on your phone is not coding on a tiny screen. It is moving work forward before you get back to your desk.”
ideas.why: This is a lower-priority V2. Only shoot it if you can show the workflow visually: issue/task → Codex starts → you walk away → progress happens. Otherwise skip it.

news.text: Claude + Canva connectors are no longer just news — they are now a live competitor wave. Treat as a wave-ride/demo opportunity.

news.text: Anthropic fellowship / frontier AI access is interesting and booker-friendly, but less directly aligned with Braydon's current highest-performing format than visual business-output demos.

news.status: Codex mobile is still a possible V2, but the first Braydon a
news.text: Codex mobile is still a possible V2, but the first Braydon attempt did not earn a priority slot. Reframe hard or skip.
Strong: Claude image assets, Claude cartoon videos, Claude Code + Codex when framed as a visible workflow.
Weak: abstract switching advice, generic “AI can help with social media,” context/memory without a concrete asset, Codex phone without a clear business payoff.
Best tactical adjustment: every idea should answer, “what does the viewer see on screen in the first 2 seconds?” If the answer is just Braydon explaining, the idea is weaker.

*Generated: May 18, 2026 at 10:51 AM MT*

---

# TikTok Ideas, May 18, 2026

**Strategist read.** The morning run was方向ally right, but the rerun is much stronger because the fresh artifact now has the thing we were missing: all 10 competitor handles, 50 competitor posts, 37 recent competitor posts, and real hook/title extraction on almost the whole evidence set. The account signal is extremely clear: Braydon's biggest recent wins are **visual-output Claude demos**. `Claude is the best AI tool` is now at **167.7K / 8,443 saves / 5.03% save**, and `Claude now makes cartoon videos` is already at **156.4K / 8,926 saves / 5.71% save**. The weaker posts are still the ones that make the value feel abstract or too small: Codex mobile is at **665 / 0.60% save**, Claude memory/context is **2,252 / 1.20%**, and social-media manager is **2,036 / 2.11%**. Today's board should therefore bias hard toward *visible business outcomes* and away from abstract “AI is changing work” takes unless the take is paired with a concrete operator use case.

The competitor board agrees. The strongest current signal is still nocode.joshua's finance-agent post at **512K / 24.5K saves**, but the best Braydon-fit lane is not “Claude killed finance.” It is “AI helps you ask better questions before you talk to your accountant.” The second cleanest signal is Sabrina's fresh Claude + Canva cluster: **41.9K / 7.00% save** and **24.9K / 8.06% save** from May 17. That stacks directly with Braydon's own new Canva post this morning, which already has the right hook/title but is still early at **640 views / 3.59% save**. The highest-confidence move today is to double down on the business-output demo lane: finance clarity, Canva/design, and Claude handoff/context workflows.

## Performance Check-In

*Post dates below are converted from UTC to Mountain Time.*

| Post Date | Views | Saves | Save Rate | On-Screen Title | Spoken Hook | Read |
|-----------|-------|-------|-----------|-----------------|-------------|------|

| May 17, 8:38 PM | 1,923 | 40 | 2.08% | Switching to Claude? Start here! 🚨 | If you've only used ChatGPT, here's the simplest way to try out Claude... | Useful but not urgent enough. Beginner-switching angle is softer than visual-output demo. |
| May 17, 12:22 PM | 2,036 | 43 | 2.11% | AI is now my social media manager 🤯🔥 | AI can now help with your social media. | Solid topic, but the first line is too generic. Needs a more specific result or before/after. |

**Top performer this snapshot:** `Claude is the best AI tool` at **167.7K / 8,443 saves**, followed closely by `Claude now makes cartoon videos` at **156.4K / 8,926 saves**. The shared pattern is not “Claude news.” It is **Claude makes a thing you can see**.

**Still climbing / worth watching:** The new Canva post is too young to judge, but the **3.59% save rate** plus Sabrina's same-day Canva evidence means the lane is alive. Do not abandon Canva because the first hour is small.

**Pattern:** Broad, visual, “watch this result appear” demos are crushing. Abstract explainers and developer-coded posts are lagging unless they are tied to a tangible business output.

## Idea 1: AI can read your money — but do not fire your accountant

- **What it's about:** Use the Claude finance-agent wave to teach business owners how AI can help them prepare better questions before talking to their bookkeeper, CPA, or financial advisor. The angle is safe, useful, and trust-building: AI is not replacing the expert; it is helping you show up smarter.
- **Evidence:** nocode.joshua's May 16 finance-agent post is the strongest current competitor signal in the scrape: **512,000 views / 24,500 saves / 4.78% save**. His on-screen title was `Claude just killed the Finance Industry`; spoken hook: “Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance AI agents...” That proves demand, but Braydon should take the more credible mentor angle.
- **On-screen title:** `AI can read your money now`
- **Spoken hook:** “AI should not replace your accountant. But it can make your next accountant meeting ten times better.”
- **Content type:** tool breakdown / myth-bust
- **Why this should work:** It wave-rides the biggest current competitor post while differentiating Braydon from the fear-based “killed finance” frame. Frank understands money, accountants, and better questions immediately. This also fits Braydon's positioning: AI empowers operators and experts instead of replacing judgment.

## Idea 2: Claude plus Canva is becoming a design department for one-person businesses

- **What it's about:** Turn the Claude + Canva connector into a practical local-business demo: flyers, event graphics, social posts, carousel drafts, and brand-consistent assets created from one prompt. Do not frame it as “make pretty graphics.” Frame it as “your tiny business can finally move like it has a design department.”
- **Evidence:** Sabrina has a fresh May 17 Claude + Canva cluster: **41.9K views / 2,934 saves / 7.00% save** with on-screen title `Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯`, and **24.9K / 2,006 saves / 8.06% save** with `Claude + Canva is a literal cheatcode 😭😭😭`. Braydon's own Canva post from 10:12 AM has the right raw material: `Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯`, 640 views, 23 saves, 3.59% save while still early.
- **On-screen title:** `Claude can be your design department`
- **Spoken hook:** “If you make flyers, carousels, menus, or event graphics, Claude just became way more useful.”

- **Why this should work:** This stacks two signals: Sabrina's fresh competitor wave and Braydon's own proven visual-output lane. It should be shot as a visible before/after demo, not a connector announcement. Show the local-business asset appearing on screen.

## Idea 3: The one context file that makes Claude stop giving generic answers

- **What it's about:** Reframe the underperforming “Claude memory/context” topic around a concrete business asset: one simple context file that tells Claude who you are, who your customer is, what you sell, your tone, your offers, and what good output looks like.
- **Evidence:** Taki's May 11 context-file post is still a strong recent signal at **18.1K views / 775 saves / 4.28% save**, on-screen title `This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately`. Braydon's own Claude memory post underperformed at **2,252 / 1.20% save**, which means the topic is useful but the first version likely did not make the payoff tangible enough.

- **Why this should work:** This is a V2/reframe, not a lazy carry-over. The new evidence is that Taki's clearer “one file” framing is outperforming Braydon's broader memory framing. Make it a simple asset viewers can copy, not a conceptual explanation of context.

## Idea 4: Claude can make the thing, but your taste still matters

- **What it's about:** Use Braydon's winning image/video posts to teach the expert-in-the-loop principle in plain English. The point is not that AI makes creative judgment irrelevant. The point is that AI gives operators more drafts, more options, and faster iteration — but the human still has to choose what is good.
- **Evidence:** Braydon's two strongest current posts are both creative-output demos: **167.7K / 5.03% save** on Claude image assets and **156.4K / 5.71% save** on cartoon videos. Rachel Woods has the operator-language pattern: “You don't need a human in the loop. You need an expert in the loop.” Her current post is early and small, so use it as language inspiration, not primary proof.

- **Why this should work:** This gives Braydon a booker-friendly thought leadership clip while still tying back to his biggest performing lane. It is not as reach-proven as the finance or Canva ideas, but it deepens the brand and explains why business owners still matter.

## Idea 5: Codex mobile is not about coding on your phone

- **What it's about:** If Braydon wants to revisit Codex mobile, change the frame completely. The value is not “code on a tiny screen.” The value is “send work forward while you are away from your desk.”
- **Evidence:** Braydon's first Codex mobile post is weak at **665 views / 4 saves / 0.60% save**, so this is not a top-priority reach play. But Marcinteodoru has a May 17 Codex-mobile-adjacent hook inside a beginner Claude post, and Braydon's earlier `Claude Code + Codex` post reached **31.3K**. The topic can work only if the use case is operator-clear.

## Today's Posting Order

1. **Main reach play:** `AI can read your money now` — strongest outside signal of the day, huge competitor proof, and a safer Braydon angle than “Claude killed finance.”
2. **Best Braydon-fit demo:** `Claude can be your design department` — stacks Sabrina's Canva cluster with Braydon's own visual-output winners. If the morning Canva post starts climbing, make this the evening V2/expanded demo.
3. **Practical save play:** `Claude needs this one file first` — good tutorial/save angle, especially if you can make the asset visible and copyable.
4. **Brand/credibility clip:** `AI still needs your taste` — good if you want a thought-leadership post that connects your big creative demos to your AI mentor philosophy.
5. **Only if there is a clear demo:** `Codex can work while you are gone` — do not force this today unless the visual workflow is strong.

## Why these ideas and not something else

- **Finance made the board** because the competitor receipt is massive and fresh: nocode.joshua at **512K** with high save volume. Braydon's differentiated frame is safer and more trustworthy than the competitor's “killed finance” hook.
- **Canva/design made the board** because it is the cleanest overlap between current competitor momentum and Braydon's own strongest pattern: visual Claude demos.
- **Context file made the board** because Braydon's own version underperformed but Taki's clearer “one file” framing is doing materially better. This is a legitimate V2, not an unearned carry-over.
- **Expert/taste made the board** because it turns Braydon's recent creative-output wins into a bigger worldview. Lower reach certainty, higher brand value.
- **Codex mobile is demoted** because Braydon's first version did not earn a second main slot. It only comes back if reframed around operator workflow, not “coding on your phone.”

| nocode.joshua | 512,000 | 24,500 | Claude just killed the Finance Industry | Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance AI agents... | Claude finance agents |
| brandnat | 279,100 | 22,452 | One person companies that feel like you hired 23 roles | So this is a secret to building one person companies that feel like you have 23 employees... | One-person company / AI employees |
| marcinteodoru | 103,500 | 5,734 | Anthropic is paying people $3,850 PER WEEK to learn AI 🤯 | If you are broke and you're trying to make some easy extra cash... | Anthropic fellowship/career opportunity |
| marcinteodoru | 42,100 | 2,478 | AI SuperAgents are beginner friendly | What the. That's how I'm opening this video. | Beginner-friendly agents |
| sabrina_ramonov | 41,900 | 2,934 | Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯 | You can now use Canva in Claude to make amazing designs, carousels, and social media posts... | Claude + Canva |
| taki.gpt | 28,400 | 1,057 | The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill | Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix... | Claude memory/handoff |
| sabrina_ramonov | 24,900 | 2,006 | Claude + Canva is a literal cheatcode 😭😭😭 | Claude plus Canva is a literal cheat code. Go to Claude Settings... | Claude + Canva |
| taki.gpt | 18,100 | 775 | This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately | Before you use Claude or any AI system, make sure that you have this one file... | Context file |
| sabrina_ramonov | 13,600 | 514 | Someone just turned Claude Code into a complete marketing department | Someone just turned Claude Code into a complete marketing department... | Claude Code marketing department |
| nocode.joshua | 10,500 | 259 | Claude x Higgsfield | Everyone is using Claude, but only 1% of users are already miles ahead... | Claude + Higgsfield / MCP |

### Hook-pattern read

- **On-screen titles are simple, concrete claims.** `Claude just killed the Finance Industry`, `Claude x Canva = $10k/month`, `This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs`. The scroll-stopper is not subtle. It names the tool and the outcome.
- **Spoken hooks add stakes or instructions.** The best hooks either ask a big question (“Did Claude just kill...”), give a direct command (“Before you use Claude...”), or promise an immediate workflow (“Go to Claude Settings...” ).
- **The caption is not the hook.** The strongest evidence came from on-screen titles and spoken hooks, not captions. Today's rerun finally has much better extraction coverage, so lean on those layers.
- **Business-output framing wins.** Finance, Canva, design assets, one-person company, context file. These are not generic AI news stories. They are “here is what AI lets an operator do now.”

### Early-catch opportunities

- **ChatGPT / AI personal finance** remains active, but it should be paired with the Claude finance-agent TikTok wave. The safer angle is “ask better questions,” not “replace your accountant.”
- **Claude + Canva connectors** are no longer just news — they are now a live competitor wave. Treat as a wave-ride/demo opportunity.
- **Anthropic fellowship / frontier AI access** is interesting and booker-friendly, but less directly aligned with Braydon's current highest-performing format than visual business-output demos.
- **Codex mobile** is still a possible V2, but the first Braydon attempt did not earn a priority slot. Reframe hard or skip.

### How your recent posts are doing

Braydon's account is currently telling a very specific story: **show me Claude making something useful and visual.** The winners are not the most technical posts. They are the posts where a viewer can immediately see the result.

- Strong: Claude image assets, Claude cartoon videos, Claude Code + Codex when framed as a visible workflow.
- Weak: abstract switching advice, generic “AI can help with social media,” context/memory without a concrete asset, Codex phone without a clear business payoff.
- Best tactical adjustment: every idea should answer, “what does the viewer see on screen in the first 2 seconds?” If the answer is just Braydon explaining, the idea is weaker.

### Hook comparison

| Braydon post | Braydon title/hook | Competitor contrast | Lesson |
|--------------|--------------------|---------------------|--------|
| Canva post | `Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯` / “Claude can now connect to Canva. Watch this.” | Sabrina: `Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯` / “make amazing designs, carousels, and social media posts” | Braydon's version is clear but could add a sharper business outcome. |
| Claude memory/context | `Claude: Give Claude a better memory for your project!` / “If Claude keeps giving you generic answers...” | Taki: `This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately` | “One file” is more concrete than “better memory.” |
| Codex mobile | `Codex can now work from your phone` / “Most people think you can only use Codex on your computer...” | Better frame: “Codex can work while you are gone” | The phone is not the benefit. Progress while away from desk is the benefit. |

## Bullet-Point Scripts

### Idea 1 — AI can read your money now

- “AI should not replace your accountant.”
- “But it can absolutely help you walk into that meeting with better questions.”
- “Look at this: finance agents can review numbers, summarize patterns, and flag things you may want to ask about.”
- “The win is not trusting AI with your money.”
- “The win is using AI to become a better business owner before you talk to the expert.”

### Idea 2 — Claude can be your design department

- “If you make flyers, carousels, menus, or event graphics, watch this.”
- “Claude can now connect directly to Canva.”
- “That means I can describe the campaign, the offer, the audience, and the style...”
- “...and start turning it into usable design assets.”
- “This is what I mean when I say AI is becoming a department, not just a chatbot.”

### Idea 3 — Claude needs this one file first

- “If Claude keeps giving you generic answers, build this before you write another prompt.”
- “It is one context file.”
- “Who you help, what you sell, your voice, your offers, your examples, and what good output looks like.”
- “Now every prompt starts with context instead of starting from zero.”
- “This is how you train Claude like a teammate instead of treating it like a search bar.”

## Sources

- TikTok scrape artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-18-1042-manual-rerun/competitors_compact.json`
- Braydon scrape artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-18-1042-manual-rerun/braydon_compact.json`
- Hook extraction manifest: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-18-1042-manual-rerun/hook_extraction_manifest.json`
- Summary artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-18-1042-manual-rerun/summary.json`

## Run notes and limitations

- Same-day rerun used the improved May 18 manual rerun artifact directory: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-18-1042-manual-rerun/`.
- Competitor scrape coverage: **50 posts across all 10 configured handles**.
- Recent competitor window: **37 posts**.
- New competitor posts: **8**.
- New-post extraction coverage: **8/8 on-screen titles**, **7/8 spoken hooks**, **7/8 both**.
- Recent-window extraction coverage: **36/37 on-screen titles**, **35/37 spoken hooks**, **34/37 both**.
- Braydon latest new post had both title and spoken hook extracted: `Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯` / “Claude can now connect to Canva. Watch this.”
- Paid transcription remained disabled; spoken hooks came from TikTok subtitle/WebVTT links where available.
- This file intentionally overwrote `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/ideas/2026-05-18-tiktok-ideas.md` instead of creating `2026-05-18-tiktok-ideas-2.md`.

briefSections.introMarkdown:
**Strategist read.** The morning run was方向ally right, but the rerun is much stronger because the fresh artifact now has the thing we were missing: all 10 competitor handles, 50 competitor posts, 37 recent competitor posts, and real hook/title extraction on almost the whole evidence set. The account signal is extremely clear: Braydon's biggest recent wins are **visual-output Claude demos**. `Claude is the best AI tool` is now at **167.7K / 8,443 saves / 5.03% save**, and `Claude now makes cartoon videos` is already at **156.4K / 8,926 saves / 5.71% save**. The weaker posts are still the ones that make the value feel abstract or too small: Codex mobile is at **665 / 0.60% save**, Claude memory/context is **2,252 / 1.20%**, and social-media manager is **2,036 / 2.11%**. Today's board should therefore bias hard toward *visible business outcomes* and away from abstract “AI is changing work” takes unless the take is paired with a concrete operator use case.

The competitor board agrees. The strongest current signal is still nocode.joshua's finance-agent post at **512K / 24.5K saves**, but the best Braydon-fit lane is not “Claude killed finance.” It is “AI helps you ask better questions before you talk to your accountant.” The second cleanest signal is Sabrina's fresh Claude + Canva cluster: **41.9K / 7.00% save** and **24.9K / 8.06% save** from May 17. That stacks directly with Braydon's own new Canva post this morning, which already has the right hook/title but is still early at **640 views / 3.59% save**. The highest-confidence move today is to double down on the business-output demo lane: finance clarity, Canva/design, and Claude handoff/context workflows.

## Performance Check-In

*Post dates below are converted from UTC to Mountain Time.*

| Post Date | Views | Saves | Save Rate | On-Screen Title | Spoken Hook | Read |
|-----------|-------|-------|-----------|-----------------|-------------|------|
| May 18, 10:12 AM | 640 | 23 | 3.59% | Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯 | Claude can now connect to Canva. Watch this. | Too early, but the save rate is acceptable and the topic is aligned with Sabrina's fresh breakout cluster. |
| May 17, 8:38 PM | 1,923 | 40 | 2.08% | Switching to Claude? Start here! 🚨 | If you've only used ChatGPT, here's the simplest way to try out Claude... | Useful but not urgent enough. Beginner-switching angle is softer than visual-output demo. |
| May 17, 12:22 PM | 2,036 | 43 | 2.11% | AI is now my social media manager 🤯🔥 | AI can now help with your social media. | Solid topic, but the first line is too generic. Needs a more specific result or before/after. |
| May 16, 10:00 PM | 156,400 | 8,926 | 5.71% | Claude now makes cartoon videos | Claude can now make amazing cartoon style videos. Watch this... | Major winner. Visual result + simple tool promise is the current account formula. |
| May 16, 8:57 AM | 2,252 | 27 | 1.20% | Claude: Give Claude a better memory for your project! 🔥 | If Claude keeps giving you generic answers... | Important topic, but it needs a sharper, more visual business-output frame. |
| May 15, 8:03 PM | 167,700 | 8,443 | 5.03% | Claude is the best AI tool | Claude can now make amazing images. Watch this. | Major winner. Again: visual output + simple demo wins. |
| May 15, 5:30 PM | 665 | 4 | 0.60% | Codex can now work from your phone 🤯🤯 | Most people think you can only use Codex on your computer... | Dead for now. The frame is too narrow and too developer-coded. |
| May 14, 5:30 PM | 31,300 | 796 | 2.54% | Claude Code + Codex = 🔥🔥🔥 | You can now use Claude Code and Codex together. Watch this. | Good reach, but weaker save rate than the visual Claude demos. |

**Top performer this snapshot:** `Claude is the best AI tool` at **167.7K / 8,443 saves**, followed closely by `Claude now makes cartoon videos` at **156.4K / 8,926 saves**. The shared pattern is not “Claude news.” It is **Claude makes a thing you can see**.

**Still climbing / worth watching:** The new Canva post is too young to judge, but the **3.59% save rate** plus Sabrina's same-day Canva evidence means the lane is alive. Do not abandon Canva because the first hour is small.

**Pattern:** Broad, visual, “watch this result appear” demos are crushing. Abstract explainers and developer-coded posts are lagging unless they are tied to a tangible business output.

briefSections.whyMarkdown:
- **Finance made the board** because the competitor receipt is massive and fresh: nocode.joshua at **512K** with high save volume. Braydon's differentiated frame is safer and more trustworthy than the competitor's “killed finance” hook.
- **Canva/design made the board** because it is the cleanest overlap between current competitor momentum and Braydon's own strongest pattern: visual Claude demos.
- **Context file made the board** because Braydon's own version underperformed but Taki's clearer “one file” framing is doing materially better. This is a legitimate V2, not an unearned carry-over.
- **Expert/taste made the board** because it turns Braydon's recent creative-output wins into a bigger worldview. Lower reach certainty, higher brand value.
- **Codex mobile is demoted** because Braydon's first version did not earn a second main slot. It only comes back if reframed around operator workflow, not “coding on your phone.”
